channel.title: Jamaica Brief on The Guyana Brief
channel.description: Recent content in Jamaica Brief on The Guyana Brief

item.title: Cousin Leroy: Yow, Dem Lock Up Jaii Frais? Wha' Gwaan Wid Dat! And Mi Nah Cosign Dem Find Oil in Yard
item.description:
Yooooo wah gwaan fam it’s your cousin Leroy calling from the BX, 174th and Morris Avenue, shoutout to everybody at the spot on White Plains Road, and I just saw the news from yard and I had to hop on here before my shift at Logan start.

Listen.

THEY LOCK UP JAII FRAIS?????

I’m sorry — THEY WHAT?

Yo I been watching Jaii Frais content for LIKE two years now. The man is a VLOGGER. A VLOGGER. He go to parties, he put out content, he’s a MEDIA personality. How you gonna lock him up since Sunday because there was a SHOOTING at the party HE WAS A GUEST AT?

item.title: Yard Report: The Vlogger, the Mayor-Chopper, the Oil We Shouldn't Want, and the Trench Town Birthday Party That Turned Into a Crime Scene
item.description:
Morning, Jamrock. Yard Report here from Kingston, processing the week the only way I know how — slowly, with black coffee, and with the grim understanding that the news will somehow get worse before I finish typing.

Let’s see what we’re working with today.

Jaii Frais: The Custody Clock Ticks Down

Popular vlogger Jaii Frais — real name Jhaedee Richards — has been in custody since Sunday following a shooting at the carnival after-party Big Wall. This morning, a Kingston and St Andrew Parish Court judge ruled that the police must either charge him or release him by 6 p.m. today.

item.title: Cousin Leroy Reports Back — Thursday, April 16, 2026
item.description: Leroy has been following the Westmoreland situation from the Bronx and has thoughts.

item.title: Yard Report — Thursday, April 16, 2026
item.description: Six months after Melissa, Westmoreland still dark. UHWI owes $40 billion in taxes. Carnival was nice though.

item.title: Jamaica Brief – Cousin Leroy: Tuesday, April 14, 2026
item.description: Cousin Leroy flew in for carnival, somehow did not know about the Big Wall shooting until Tuesday, and has questions about Negril's ambulance situation.

item.title: Jamaica Brief – Yard Report: Tuesday, April 14, 2026
item.description: Carnival ended with gunshots, a boy got shot by a cop on a rooftop, the justice minister told JPs to stop profiling, and Domino's has a new owner. Tuesday's Kingston dispatch.

item.title: Cousin Leroy Report — Sunday, April 12, 2026
item.description: Cousin Leroy is watching Road March from the Bronx on his phone, upset about the Negril ambulance situation, and has opinions about the oil price and the budget.

item.title: Yard Report — Sunday, April 12, 2026
item.description: Road March Sunday in Kingston, Negril still has no ambulance, a Jamaican referee is going to the World Cup, and the budget's oil price assumption is looking embarrassing.

item.title: Cousin Leroy Report — Saturday, April 11, 2026
item.description: Cousin Leroy, calling in from the Bronx, has thoughts on Bunny Shaw's hat-trick, the Maryland plaque ceremony, and why he hasn't been back to Spanish Town in a while.

item.title: Yard Report — Saturday, April 11, 2026
item.description: Spanish Town mourns a teenager lost in a house fire, 71 dead on the roads already this year, and Bunny Shaw scores a hat-trick while the government hands out plaques in Maryland.

item.title: Jamaica Brief — Cousin Leroy, April 10, 2026
item.description: Leroy in the Bronx has thoughts about Shanoya, the soldier situation, gas station robberies, and why Jamaica keep making him proud and worried at the same time.

item.title: Jamaica Brief — Yard Report, April 10, 2026
item.description: JDF soldier charged with girlfriend's murder, bartender shot in St. Elizabeth, Shanoya Douglas has world leads, and an MP has been summoned. Another Friday in Jamaica.

item.title: Cousin Leroy's Jamaica Update – Thursday, April 9, 2026
item.description: Cousin Leroy in the Bronx reads the Jamaica news and has some thoughts.

item.title: The Yard Report – Thursday, April 9, 2026
item.description: Dispatches from Kingston — gas going up, hurricane victims still in schools, and Jamaica not in the World Cup. A normal week.

item.title: Cousin Leroy Seh — Jamaica, Monday April 6, 2026
item.description: Cousin Leroy, calling from the Bronx, is very proud of January's crime numbers and not worried about the fuel price.

item.title: The Yard Report — Jamaica Brief, Monday April 6, 2026
item.description: Jamaica's weekly news with the bark left on. JACDEN scandal, fuel prices up 20%, and Jamaica still cleaning up after Melissa.
